name: Hydrogen
real_world_summary: Hydrogen (H, Z=1) is a gas nonmetal element. It is the primary fusion fuel in stars and a critical propellant feedstock in many aerospace fuel cycles.
lore_hook: Frontier fleets route this element into life-support chemistry, fuel balancing, and biosphere sustainment loops.

name: Helium
real_world_summary: Helium (He, Z=2) is a gas noble gas element. It is used in cryogenics and low-temperature superconducting systems, and it leaks from planetary atmospheres over geologic time.
lore_hook: Captured stocks stabilize cryogenic labs, inert manufacturing bays, and precision propulsion calibration runs.

name: Lithium
real_world_summary: Lithium (Li, Z=3) is a solid alkali metal element. It is central to high-energy battery chemistry and is consumed in strategic energy storage industries.
lore_hook: Guild refiners treat this as a volatile process feedstock traded through tightly regulated industrial markets.

name: Beryllium
real_world_summary: Beryllium (Be, Z=4) is a solid alkaline earth metal element. It combines low density with high stiffness, making it useful for precision structures and optical mounts.
lore_hook: Colonial foundries use this element in structural compounds and habitat resilience upgrades.

name: Boron
real_world_summary: Boron (B, Z=5) is a solid metalloid element. Boron compounds are widely used in neutron absorption, ceramics, and high-temperature composites.
lore_hook: Signal foundries route this element into sensors, processors, and guidance-grade control packages.

name: Carbon
real_world_summary: Carbon (C, Z=6) is a solid nonmetal element. Carbon forms graphite, diamond, and advanced carbon-fiber structures used across aerospace systems.
lore_hook: Frontier fleets route this element into life-support chemistry, fuel balancing, and biosphere sustainment loops.

name: Nitrogen
real_world_summary: Nitrogen (N, Z=7) is a gas nonmetal element. Nitrogen dominates many atmospheres and is essential for inert processing and industrial synthesis chains.
lore_hook: Frontier fleets route this element into life-support chemistry, fuel balancing, and biosphere sustainment loops.

name: Oxygen
real_world_summary: Oxygen (O, Z=8) is a gas nonmetal element. Oxygen supports oxidation chemistry and high-performance oxidizer cycles in propulsion systems.
lore_hook: Frontier fleets route this element into life-support chemistry, fuel balancing, and biosphere sustainment loops.

name: Fluorine
real_world_summary: Fluorine (F, Z=9) is a gas halogen element. It appears in naturally occurring minerals and has specialized roles in modern materials and industrial chemistry.
lore_hook: Chemical syndicates move this element through oxidizer, etching, and sterilization supply chains.

name: Neon
real_world_summary: Neon (Ne, Z=10) is a gas noble gas element. It appears in naturally occurring minerals and has specialized roles in modern materials and industrial chemistry.
lore_hook: Captured stocks stabilize cryogenic labs, inert manufacturing bays, and precision propulsion calibration runs.

name: Sodium
real_world_summary: Sodium (Na, Z=11) is a solid alkali metal element. Sodium appears in salts and specialized heat-transfer systems in certain industrial reactor concepts.
lore_hook: Guild refiners treat this as a volatile process feedstock traded through tightly regulated industrial markets.

name: Magnesium
real_world_summary: Magnesium (Mg, Z=12) is a solid alkaline earth metal element. Magnesium enables lightweight alloys and structural mass reduction in aerospace manufacturing.
lore_hook: Colonial foundries use this element in structural compounds and habitat resilience upgrades.

name: Aluminum
real_world_summary: Aluminum (Al, Z=13) is a solid post-transition metal element. Aluminum is a core structural metal in spacecraft, pressure vessels, and industrial fabrication.
lore_hook: Shipyards consume this element for armored hullwork, reactor internals, and high-wear components.

name: Silicon
real_world_summary: Silicon (Si, Z=14) is a solid metalloid element. Silicon is foundational for semiconductors, photovoltaic systems, and control electronics.
lore_hook: Silicon streams feed quantum-control fabs and every major shipboard compute stack.

name: Phosphorus
real_world_summary: Phosphorus (P, Z=15) is a solid nonmetal element. Phosphorus chemistry is used in fertilizers, energetic compounds, and some high-performance materials.
lore_hook: Frontier fleets route this element into life-support chemistry, fuel balancing, and biosphere sustainment loops.

name: Sulfur
real_world_summary: Sulfur (S, Z=16) is a solid nonmetal element. Sulfur is common in sulfide minerals and industrial chemical processing chains.
lore_hook: Frontier fleets route this element into life-support chemistry, fuel balancing, and biosphere sustainment loops.

name: Chlorine
real_world_summary: Chlorine (Cl, Z=17) is a gas halogen element. Chlorine chemistry is important for industrial synthesis, sterilization, and some propellant families.
lore_hook: Chemical syndicates move this element through oxidizer, etching, and sterilization supply chains.

name: Argon
real_world_summary: Argon (Ar, Z=18) is a gas noble gas element. Argon is an inert shielding gas and is used in electric propulsion test and process environments.
lore_hook: Captured stocks stabilize cryogenic labs, inert manufacturing bays, and precision propulsion calibration runs.

name: Potassium
real_world_summary: Potassium (K, Z=19) is a solid alkali metal element. Potassium is abundant in silicate geology and appears in specialty heat transfer and chemical systems.
lore_hook: Guild refiners treat this as a volatile process feedstock traded through tightly regulated industrial markets.

name: Calcium
real_world_summary: Calcium (Ca, Z=20) is a solid alkaline earth metal element. Calcium compounds dominate many rocky mineral systems and construction chemistries.
lore_hook: Colonial foundries use this element in structural compounds and habitat resilience upgrades.

name: Scandium
real_world_summary: Scandium (Sc, Z=21) is a solid transition metal element. It appears in naturally occurring minerals and has specialized roles in modern materials and industrial chemistry.
lore_hook: Shipyards consume this element for armored hullwork, reactor internals, and high-wear components.

name: Titanium
real_world_summary: Titanium (Ti, Z=22) is a solid transition metal element. Titanium provides excellent strength-to-weight performance and corrosion resistance in aerospace structures.
lore_hook: SpaceShift fleets favor titanium-rich feeds for hull lattice upgrades and long-duration expedition frames.

name: Vanadium
real_world_summary: Vanadium (V, Z=23) is a solid transition metal element. It appears in naturally occurring minerals and has specialized roles in modern materials and industrial chemistry.

name: Meitnerium
real_world_summary: Meitnerium (Mt, Z=109) is a solid transition metal element. It appears in naturally occurring minerals and has specialized roles in modern materials and industrial chemistry.
lore_hook: Shipyards consume this element for armored hullwork, reactor internals, and high-wear components.
